annotations.label: Riemannian manifold (informal)

anchor.snippet: a Riemannian manifold (or Riemann space ) is a geometric space on which many geometric notions such as distance, angles, length, volume, and curvature are defined

annotations.note: `IsRiemannianManifold I M` is the Prop-valued typeclass registering the Riemannian-manifold predicate on a manifold equipped with an emetric structure and a Riemannian bundle on its tangent bundle.

annotations.label: Riemannian metric (informal)

anchor.snippet: a Riemannian metric (or just a metric ) on a smooth manifold is a smoothly varying choice of inner product for each tangent space

annotations.note: `ContMDiffRiemannianMetric IB n F E` is a family of inner products on the fibers of a vector bundle varying `C^n`-smoothly with the base point; applied to the tangent bundle it is exactly a Riemannian metric.

annotations.label: Theorema Egregium

anchor.snippet: the Theorema Egregium says that the Gaussian curvature is an intrinsic property of surfaces

annotations.note: Gaussian curvature of surfaces is not formalized in Mathlib, so Theorema Egregium is absent.

annotations.label: Local isometry (surfaces)

anchor.snippet: A map that preserves the local measurements of a surface is called a local isometry

annotations.note: No definition of local isometry between Riemannian manifolds/surfaces exists in Mathlib; only the metric-space `Isometry` predicate is available.

annotations.label: Intrinsic/extrinsic property

anchor.snippet: A property of a surface is called an intrinsic property if it is preserved by local isometries and it is called an extrinsic property if it is not

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of intrinsic vs extrinsic property of a surface.

annotations.label: Riemannian metric and Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: A Riemannian metric

annotations.note: Anchor tightened to point to the definitional sentence.
annotations.note_annotation: `Bundle.RiemannianMetric E` gives a family of inner products on the fibers of a vector bundle (and `IsRiemannianManifold` packages the manifold-level assertion).

annotations.label: Metric components in coordinates

anchor.snippet: one can define the Riemannian metric's components at each point

annotations.note: Mathlib's Riemannian metrics are coordinate-free bilinear forms on fibers; the classical `g_{ij}` coordinate components are not defined.

annotations.label: Continuous/smooth Riemannian metric

anchor.snippet: is continuous if its components

annotations.note: `IsContinuousRiemannianBundle` and `IsContMDiffRiemannianBundle` express, respectively, that the inner product on fibers varies continuously / `C^n`-smoothly with the base point.

annotations.label: Musical isomorphism

anchor.snippet: a Riemannian metric induces an isomorphism of bundles between the tangent bundle and the cotangent bundle

annotations.note: The pointwise `InnerProductSpace.toDual` gives the fiberwise flat isomorphism of a Hilbert space with its dual, but the bundle-level musical isomorphism between the tangent and cotangent bundles is not built in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isometry

anchor.snippet: An isometry is a function between Riemannian manifolds which preserves all of the structure of Riemannian manifolds

annotations.note: Anchor changed to the primary definitional sentence; the previous snippet 'a diffeomorphism' was too generic.
annotations.note_annotation: Mathlib's `Isometry` for (pseudo)emetric spaces applies at the level of the induced distance, but there is no dedicated `RiemannianIsometry` preserving the smooth structure and the metric tensor.

annotations.label: Local isometry

anchor.snippet: is a local isometry if every

annotations.note: No local-isometry notion for Riemannian manifolds is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riemannian volume form

anchor.snippet: called the Riemannian volume form

annotations.note: The Riemannian volume form on an oriented Riemannian manifold is not defined in Mathlib; only the pointwise `Orientation.volumeForm` on a single inner product space exists.

annotations.label: Euclidean metric

anchor.snippet: The (canonical) Euclidean metric

annotations.note: `riemannianMetricVectorSpace F` is the canonical Riemannian metric on an inner product space given by its inner product on each tangent space, and `EuclideanSpace` is the standard inner-product model.

annotations.label: Riemannian submanifold

anchor.snippet: is said to be a Riemannian submanifold

annotations.note: Anchor sharpened to the defining phrase.
annotations.note_annotation: Riemannian submanifolds and the induced metric are not formalized.

annotations.label: Round metric on the sphere

anchor.snippet: The Riemannian metric this induces on

annotations.note: The round Riemannian metric on the sphere is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ellipsoid as submanifold

anchor.snippet: The ellipsoid

annotations.note: Ellipsoids as Riemannian submanifolds are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graph of smooth function

anchor.snippet: The graph of a smooth function

annotations.note: The graph of a smooth function as an embedded Riemannian submanifold is not treated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Covering space inherits metric

anchor.snippet: any smooth covering space of a Riemannian manifold inherits a Riemannian metric

annotations.note: Mathlib does not construct a Riemannian metric on a smooth covering space by pullback.

annotations.label: Isometric immersion/embedding

anchor.snippet: is called an isometric immersion (or isometric embedding )

annotations.note: Isometric immersions/embeddings between Riemannian manifolds are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Product Riemannian metric

anchor.snippet: naturally put a Riemannian metric

annotations.note: The product Riemannian metric on a product of Riemannian manifolds is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flat torus

anchor.snippet: is called the flat torus

annotations.note: Anchor tightened to the naming phrase.
annotations.note_annotation: The flat torus as a Riemannian manifold is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Positive combinations of metrics

anchor.snippet: is another Riemannian metric on

annotations.note: Anchor moved to the conclusion of the proposition.
annotations.note_annotation: No lemma stating that positive linear combinations of Riemannian metrics remain Riemannian is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of Riemannian metric

anchor.section: Every smooth manifold admits a Riemannian metric
anchor.snippet: Every smooth manifold admits a (non-canonical) Riemannian metric

annotations.note: Mathlib only constructs the canonical Riemannian metric on inner product spaces; the general existence result for arbitrary smooth manifolds (via partitions of unity) is not there.

annotations.label: Nash embedding theorem

anchor.section: Every smooth manifold admits a Riemannian metric
anchor.snippet: the Nash embedding theorem states that, given any smooth Riemannian manifold

annotations.note: The Nash embedding theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Admissible curve and length

anchor.snippet: An admissible curve is a piecewise smooth curve

annotations.note: `Manifold.pathELength` gives the length integral of a `C^1` path in a manifold whose tangent spaces have an `ENorm`; piecewise-smooth admissible curves are subsumed as `C^1` paths.

annotations.label: Riemannian distance is a metric

anchor.snippet: is a metric space , and the metric topology on

annotations.note: `EMetricSpace.ofRiemannianMetric` builds an emetric space structure whose topology agrees with the manifold's, using `riemannianEDist`; positivity/triangle inequality are proved but only an `ℝ≥0∞`-valued distance is provided.

annotations.label: Diameter

anchor.snippet: The diameter of the metric space

annotations.note: `Metric.diam` is defined for arbitrary (pseudo)metric spaces, subsuming the diameter of a Riemannian manifold under its induced distance.

annotations.label: Compactness iff finite diameter (complete)

anchor.snippet: is complete, then it is compact if and only if it has finite diameter

annotations.note: Anchor sharpened from the generic 'If' to the actual proposition text.
annotations.note_annotation: The Riemannian statement 'complete ∧ finite diameter ↔ compact' (invoking Hopf–Rinow) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine connection

anchor.snippet: An (affine) connection is an additional structure on a Riemannian manifold that defines differentiation of one vector field with respect to another

annotations.note: `CovariantDerivative I F V` is a bundled Koszul connection on a vector bundle; taking `V = TangentSpace I` recovers an affine connection on a manifold.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative

anchor.snippet: is called the covariant derivative of

annotations.note: `CovariantDerivative` is Mathlib's bundled covariant-derivative structure on a vector bundle over a manifold.

annotations.label: Metric-preserving connection

anchor.snippet: is said to preserve the metric if

annotations.note: `CovariantDerivative.IsMetricCompatible cov` states that a connection on a Riemannian bundle preserves the metric (its `derivMetricTensor` vanishes).

annotations.label: Torsion-free connection

anchor.snippet: is torsion-free if

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `CovariantDerivative.torsion` on the tangent bundle and characterizes torsion-freeness via `torsion_eq_zero_iff` (⇔ `∇_X Y - ∇_Y X = [X,Y]`).

annotations.label: Levi-Civita connection existence/uniqueness

anchor.snippet: A Levi-Civita connection is a torsion-free connection that preserves the metric. Once a Riemannian metric is fixed, there exists a unique Levi-Civita connection

annotations.note: Although metric-compatibility and torsion-freeness are defined, existence/uniqueness of the Levi-Civita connection is not proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vector field along a curve

anchor.snippet: a smooth vector field along

annotations.note: No definition of a smooth vector field along a curve in a manifold appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Extension of vector field along curve

anchor.snippet: is called an extension of

annotations.note: No notion of extending a vector field along a curve is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative along a curve

anchor.snippet: there is a unique operator

annotations.note: The existence/uniqueness of the covariant derivative operator along a curve is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic

anchor.snippet: is called a geodesic

annotations.note: Geodesics on Riemannian manifolds are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence and uniqueness of geodesics

anchor.snippet: there exists a geodesic

annotations.note: The local existence and uniqueness of geodesics is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximal geodesic

anchor.snippet: one obtains a geodesic called a maximal geodesic

annotations.note: Maximal geodesics are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shortest curves are geodesics

anchor.snippet: Every curve

annotations.note: The result identifying length-minimizing curves with geodesics is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geodesics in the Euclidean plane

anchor.snippet: The nonconstant maximal geodesics of the Euclidean plane

annotations.note: Without a geodesic definition, the identification of Euclidean geodesics as affine lines is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geodesics on the sphere

anchor.snippet: are exactly the great circles

annotations.note: The identification of great circles as sphere geodesics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesically complete

anchor.snippet: is geodesically complete if the domain of every maximal geodesic is

annotations.note: Geodesic completeness is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hopf–Rinow theorem

anchor.snippet: The Hopf–Rinow theorem characterizes geodesically complete manifolds

annotations.note: Anchor moved from the generic 'Let' to the theorem's characterizing sentence.
annotations.note_annotation: The Hopf–Rinow theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Parallel vector field

anchor.snippet: call a smooth vector field

annotations.note: Parallel vector fields along a curve are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Parallel transport

anchor.snippet: to parallel transport a vector

annotations.note: Parallel transport is not defined in Mathlib (the file `CovariantDerivative/Metric.lean` even notes it as a TODO).

annotations.label: Riemann curvature tensor

anchor.snippet: The Riemann curvature tensor is the map

annotations.note: The Riemann curvature tensor is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vanishing curvature iff locally Euclidean

anchor.snippet: The Riemann curvature tensor is 0 at every point if and only if the manifold is locally isometric to Euclidean space

annotations.note: Not formalized: neither the curvature tensor nor local Riemannian isometries are in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ricci curvature tensor

anchor.snippet: The Ricci curvature tensor is

annotations.note: The Ricci curvature tensor is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Einstein metric / Einstein manifold

anchor.snippet: is called an Einstein metric if Einstein's equation

annotations.note: Einstein manifolds are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constant curvature

anchor.snippet: A Riemannian manifold is said to have constant curvature κ if every sectional curvature equals the number κ

annotations.note: Sectional and constant curvatures are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constant curvature implies Einstein

anchor.snippet: every Riemannian manifold of constant curvature is an Einstein manifold

annotations.note: Not in Mathlib: prerequisites (constant curvature, Einstein) are missing.

annotations.label: Riemannian space form

anchor.snippet: A Riemannian space form is a Riemannian manifold with constant curvature which is additionally connected and geodesically complete

annotations.note: Riemannian space forms are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Killing–Hopf theorem

anchor.snippet: the Killing–Hopf theorem says that any simply connected spherical space form is homothetic to the sphere

annotations.note: The Killing–Hopf classification of space forms is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left-invariant metric on Lie group

anchor.snippet: the inner product on the tangent space at an arbitrary point p is defined by

annotations.note: Left-invariant Riemannian metrics on Lie groups are not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bi-invariant metric existence

anchor.snippet: a Lie group can be given a bi-invariant Riemannian metric if and only if it is the product of a compact Lie group with an abelian Lie group

annotations.note: The characterization of Lie groups admitting a bi-invariant metric is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homogeneous Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: is said to be homogeneous if for every pair of points x and y in M , there is some isometry f of the Riemannian manifold sending x to y

annotations.note: Homogeneous Riemannian manifolds are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homogeneous implies geodesically complete

anchor.snippet: Every homogeneous Riemannian manifold is geodesically complete and has constant scalar curvature

annotations.note: Not in Mathlib: homogeneity, geodesic completeness, and scalar curvature are all missing.

annotations.label: Classification of homogeneous Riemannian manifolds

anchor.snippet: Up to isometry, all homogeneous Riemannian manifolds arise by the following construction

annotations.note: The Lie-theoretic classification of homogeneous Riemannian manifolds is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: is said to be symmetric if for every point p of M there exists some isometry of the manifold with p as a fixed point and for which the negation of the differential at p is the identity map

annotations.note: Riemannian symmetric spaces are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cartan: locally symmetric implies symmetric

anchor.snippet: Élie Cartan proved in the 1920s that a locally symmetric Riemannian manifold which is geodesically complete and simply-connected must in fact be symmetric

annotations.note: Cartan's local-to-global theorem for symmetric spaces is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Berger's holonomy classification

anchor.snippet: any Riemannian manifold which is simply connected and irreducible is either a symmetric space or has Riemannian holonomy belonging to a list of only seven possibilities

annotations.note: Berger's holonomy classification is not in Mathlib (no holonomy group is defined).

annotations.label: Weak/strong Riemannian metric

anchor.snippet: A weak Riemannian metric on

annotations.note: Weak vs strong Riemannian metrics on infinite-dimensional manifolds are not treated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hilbert space strong metric

anchor.snippet: is a strong Riemannian metric

annotations.note: This infinite-dimensional example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: L² weak metric on diffeomorphism group

anchor.snippet: weak Riemannian metric on

annotations.note: The L² weak metric on a diffeomorphism group is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strong metric separates points

anchor.snippet: is a strong Riemannian metric on

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vanishing geodesic distance for weak metric

anchor.snippet: may fail to separate points. In fact, it may even be identically 0

annotations.note: The Michor–Mumford vanishing-distance phenomenon is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hopf–Rinow (strong, infinite-dim)

anchor.snippet: metric completeness (in the metric

annotations.note: The infinite-dimensional Hopf–Rinow theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangent space

anchor.snippet: there is an associated vector space

annotations.note: `TangentSpace I x` is Mathlib's tangent space of a manifold at a point.

annotations.label: Induced norm from Riemannian metric

anchor.snippet: This induces a norm

annotations.note: Under `[RiemannianBundle E]`, Mathlib installs a `NormedAddCommGroup (E b)` and `InnerProductSpace ℝ (E b)` on each fiber via `RiemannianMetric.toCore`, giving the induced norm on tangent spaces.

annotations.label: Riemannian volume form preserved by orientation-preserving isometries

anchor.snippet: The Riemannian volume form is preserved by orientation-preserving isometries

annotations.note: The Riemannian volume form is not defined in Mathlib, so its invariance under orientation-preserving isometries is not stated.

annotations.label: Volume of a compact Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: the volume of

annotations.note: The Riemannian volume of a compact Riemannian manifold is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pullback Riemannian metric

anchor.snippet: The pullback

annotations.note: Pullback of a Riemannian metric along an immersion is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: n-sphere as embedded submanifold

anchor.snippet: is a smooth embedded submanifold of Euclidean space

annotations.note: The n-sphere as a smooth embedded Riemannian submanifold of Euclidean space is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Product of Euclidean lines is Euclidean

anchor.snippet: is isometric to

annotations.note: The Riemannian isometry `ℝ × ⋯ × ℝ ≃ ℝⁿ` is not formalized; only the linear/inner-product identification via `EuclideanSpace` exists.

annotations.label: Riemannian distance function

anchor.snippet: For

annotations.note: `Manifold.riemannianEDist I x y` is the infimum of `pathELength` over `C^1` paths between `x` and `y`, i.e., the Riemannian (extended) distance.

annotations.label: Whitney embedding gives a Riemannian metric

anchor.section: Every smooth manifold admits a Riemannian metric
anchor.snippet: An alternative proof uses the Whitney embedding theorem to embed

annotations.note: Neither the Whitney embedding theorem for smooth manifolds nor the resulting induced Riemannian metric is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constant curvature: Ricci and scalar formulas

anchor.snippet: This implies that the Ricci curvature is given by R jk = ( n − 1) κg jk and the scalar curvature is n ( n − 1) κ

annotations.note: No curvature tensors are defined in Mathlib, so these formulas are not stated.

annotations.label: Left-invariant metrics have constant scalar curvature

anchor.snippet: All left-invariant metrics have constant scalar curvature

annotations.note: Left-invariant metrics and scalar curvature are both absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bi-invariant metrics have nonnegative sectional curvature

anchor.snippet: Any bi-invariant Riemannian metric on a Lie group has nonnegative sectional curvature

annotations.note: Sectional curvature and bi-invariant metrics are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric spaces are homogeneous

anchor.snippet: Every Riemannian symmetric space is homogeneous

annotations.note: Neither symmetric nor homogeneous Riemannian manifolds are defined in Mathlib.
